claim: Okamoto 1987 §2.1 (p. 318) defines simple roots α_1=e_1−e_2, α_2=e_2 of B_2 with bilinear form (e_i|e_j)=δ_{ij}; reflections s_1: v↦(v_2,v_1), s_2: v↦(v_1,−v_2), s_0: v↦(−1−v_2,−1−v_1) generate G ≅ W^{aff}(B_2)

Sakai 2001 Add 6 (p. 40–41) tabulates D_6^{(1)⊥} = (2A_1)^{(1)} with simple roots α_1=E_4−E_5, α_0=3E_0−E_1−E_2−E_3−2E_4−E_6−E_7−E_8−E_9, α'_0=E_6−E_7, α'_1=3E_0−E_1−...−E_5−2E_6−E_8−E_9 in the Picard lattice of the rational E_8^{(1)}-surface

Sakai 2001 Table 6 (p. 31) records the Cremona action of the D_6^{(1)}-surface as Aut(D_6^{(1)}) ⋉ W((2A_1)^{(1)}); equations of motion (16)–(18) impose level constraint a_0+a_1=b_0+b_1=1 (after rescaling ds/dt=4)

Both groups are infinite (|W^{aff}(B_2)|=∞, |W((2A_1)^{(1)})|=∞), hence the spec's H_1 cardinality argument does not apply directly; the natural construction is at the level of affine Weyl groups with rank-2 translation lattice on each side and a 4-element finite quotient on each side

Hypothesis H_3* selected: φ is an injective homomorphism W^{aff}(B_2) ↪ Aut(D_6^{(1)}) ⋉ W((2A_1)^{(1)}) defined on generators by s_1↦σ, s_2↦r'_0, s_0↦σππ' under the parameter identification a_0=−v_1, a_1=1+v_1, b_0=−v_2, b_1=1+v_2

Sympy verification confirms parameter-action compatibility for all 6 single-generator and length-2 words {s_0, s_1, s_2, s_1 s_2, s_2 s_0, s_1 s_0}; each row of the diagram (Okamoto v ↔ Sakai params) commutes; verify_homomorphism.log lines 11–22 record all six rows passing

All six defining relations of W^{aff}(B_2) are satisfied by φ on (a_0,a_1,b_0,b_1): s_1²=s_2²=s_0²=e, (s_1 s_2)^4=e, (s_2 s_0)^4=e, (s_1 s_0)²=e; sympy returns each phi-image word equal to the initial state (verify_homomorphism.log lines 24–35)

Okamoto's auxiliary involution y: v↦−v (slot 07 §2.3, p. 320) lies in the image of φ as the Weyl element r_0 r'_0 = φ(s_1 s_2 s_1 s_2) = φ((s_1 s_2)²); sympy match confirmed (verify_homomorphism.log line 38)

Image of φ has index 2 in Aut(D_6^{(1)}) ⋉ W((2A_1)^{(1)}); the cokernel Z/2 is generated by the one-sided leaf swap π of (2A_1)^{(1)}, corresponding on the Okamoto side to the Bäcklund involution (v_1, v_2) ↦ (−1−v_1, v_2) which is NOT in W^{aff}(B_2) generated by {s_0, s_1, s_2}; sympy match confirmed (verify_homomorphism.log line 41)

Kernel of φ is trivial: faithfulness of the codomain action on the parameter slice {a_0+a_1=b_0+b_1=1} plus faithfulness of W^{aff}(B_2) action on V (Okamoto Theorem 1) imply ker(φ)={e}; consequently the SES 1 → W^{aff}(B_2) →φ Aut(D_6^{(1)}) ⋉ W((2A_1)^{(1)}) → Z/2 → 1 is exact

Cross-walk table (crosswalk_table.md) lists 11 rows: 3 Okamoto generators + 4 length-2 words + 1 auxiliary involution y + 2 derived Weyl reflections (r_1, r'_1 obtained as conjugates) + 2 cokernel rows (π, π'); each row is consistent with Sakai's standard W((2A_1)^{(1)}) action and with KNY 2017 (slot 14) Bäcklund formulae

KNY 2017 cross-check: the 11 cross-walk rows do not contradict any explicit Bäcklund relation in slot 14; KNY 2017 records the same r_0/r_1/r'_0/r'_1/σ formulae but does NOT state the homomorphism φ to W^{aff}(B_2) (slot 14 contains 0 occurrences of 'B_2' as a Lie-group symbol, per prompt 029 readback); halt-condition HALT_CONTRADICTS_KNY_2017 is NOT triggered

SIARC-primary authorship: the homomorphism φ in this form is not stated verbatim in any of the audited post-Sakai sources (slot 07 Okamoto 1987, slot 13 Sakai 1999/2001, slot 14 KNY 2017, slot 30 NY 2004 + KNY/NY OA preprints, slot for FW 2005 substitute, Conte-Musette NIA per prompt 028); authorship of φ is SIARC-derived this session (2026-05-04)
